_thread: Thread B — AWS AppConfig feature flag configuration
_notes: Clean Q&A thread. Schema fits well with config type. incident-centric fields handled correctly with null/empty.
title: Configuring Feature Flags for Pre-Prod Environment in AWS AppConfig
summary: The staging environment was renamed 'pre-prod' in AWS AppConfig ~3 months ago — this causes confusion when engineers look for a 'staging' environment. To enable a feature flag: open the Feature Flag config profile for the target environment, set the flag to enabled, save a new version, and start a deployment using the AllAtOnce strategy for internal testing. Rollback is available via the 'Stop deployment and roll back' button during an active deployment, or by setting the flag to disabled and redeploying. Deploy access requires the `appconfig:StartDeployment` IAM permission scoped to the target environment, granted via the platform-eng-deployers group.
prerequisites: `appconfig:StartDeployment` IAM permission on the target environment (granted via platform-eng-deployers group)
steps_taken: Locate the pre-prod environment in AWS AppConfig (previously named 'staging') under the app's Environments tab
Open the Feature Flag configuration profile for pre-prod
Set `smart-search-v2` flag to enabled and save a new version
Start a new deployment using the AllAtOnce deployment strategy
Verify environment selector is set to pre-prod before deploying
Confirm flag is live and test
resolution: Flag smart-search-v2 enabled in pre-prod via AWS AppConfig deployment. Confirmed working by requester.
action_items: Add feature flag configuration process to platform docs
Consider adding a guard rail or confirmation prompt on environment selection before deployment
